# Chat PDF — GUI Wireframes
**Step 02: Design GUI Pages**

This document captures all screen states for the Chat PDF desktop application (PyQt6).

---

## Wireframe Overview

![Chat PDF Wireframes](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Screen Descriptions

### Screen 1 — No PDF Loaded (Initial State)

The default state when the app launches.

| Zone | Component | State |
|------|-----------|-------|
| Toolbar | "Upload PDF" button | Enabled |
| Toolbar | Filename badge | Shows "No PDF loaded" (placeholder) |
| Toolbar | "Clear" button | Disabled |
| Chat area | Empty state | Icon + "Upload a PDF to start chatting" |
| Input bar | Text input | Disabled |
| Input bar | Send button | Disabled |

---

### Screen 2 — PDF Loaded, Active Conversation

Activated once a PDF is successfully uploaded and parsed.

| Zone | Component | State |
|------|-----------|-------|
| Toolbar | "Upload PDF" button | Enabled (replace current PDF) |
| Toolbar | Filename badge | Shows loaded filename (e.g. `annual_report_2024.pdf`) |
| Toolbar | "Clear" button | Enabled |
| Chat area | User bubbles | Right-aligned, blue background |
| Chat area | AI bubbles | Left-aligned, neutral background |
| Chat area | Typing indicator | 3-dot animation shown while API call is in-flight |
| Input bar | Text input | Enabled |
| Input bar | Send button | Enabled; disabled again while request is in-flight |

---

### Screen 2b — PDF Load Error (Status Bar)

Shown when the selected PDF cannot be parsed (corrupted, password-protected, etc.).

| Zone | Component | State |
|------|-----------|-------|
| Status bar | Error message | Red strip below toolbar with ⚠ icon and description |
| Status bar | Dismiss button | ✕ — dismisses the bar; bar also clears on next successful PDF load |
| Chat area | Empty state | Unchanged — no PDF is loaded |
| Input bar | Text input + Send | Remain disabled |

> **Behaviour:** The status bar appears between the toolbar and the chat area.
> It persists until the user dismisses it or successfully loads a new PDF.

---

### Screen 2c — API / Chat Error (Inline Bubble)

Shown when an OpenAI API call fails (network error, timeout, invalid key, etc.).

| Zone | Component | State |
|------|-----------|-------|
| Chat area | Error bubble | Left-aligned red bubble inline in the conversation stream |
| Input bar | Text input + Send | Re-enabled after error so user can retry |

> **Behaviour:** The error message appears as a chat bubble directly after the
> user's message that triggered it. No modal or separate dialog is used.

---

## Error Handling Summary

| Error Type | Where Displayed | Dismissible |
|------------|----------------|-------------|
| PDF failed to load | Status bar (below toolbar) | Yes — ✕ button |
| API / network failure | Inline red chat bubble | No (part of conversation history) |
| No PDF loaded (user tries to send) | Send button stays disabled | N/A |

---

## Design Decisions

- **Single window** — no separate dialogs or panels; everything happens in one view.
- **Disabled state** for input/Send prevents user confusion when no PDF is loaded.
- **Inline errors** for chat failures keep the conversation flow intact.
- **Status bar** for PDF errors gives persistent, non-intrusive feedback without blocking the UI.
- **Typing indicator** (3 dots) communicates that a response is being fetched.

---

*Generated as part of the Chat PDF app design process — Step 02 of 16.*
